description_long: This object is a roughly triangular, elongated stone with a hollowed-out core running through its length, visible from both ends. The surface is marked with a series of shallow, intersecting grooves and incised lines, giving it a textured, patterned appearance. The stone is light beige to tan in color, with some areas showing subtle variations in tone and faint surface discoloration. One side of the stone is broader and flatter, while the opposite end tapers to a more rounded, blunt point. The hollow channel appears irregular and roughly carved, suggesting it may have served a functional purpose. The overall form and markings indicate it could have been used as a primitive tool, weight, or decorative element in an urban or garden setting.
description: A beige, triangular stone with a hollow core and incised surface patterns, featuring a rough, textured finish and visible grooves.
description_view_0: The stone appears broad and triangular with visible incised lines and a flat surface, showing the full width and surface patterning.
description_view_1: The elongated, oval profile is visible, with the hollow core running through the center and some writing or markings on the surface.
description_view_2: The opposite broad side is shown, with more incised lines and a slightly more rounded, blunt end, as well as visible surface texture.
description_view_3: The hollowed-out core is clearly visible from this end-on view, showing the irregular, roughly carved opening.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or partially embedded in the ground
attribute_pattern: Intersecting incised lines and grooves forming a geometric, irregular pattern across the surface.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and shows signs of minor discoloration and abrasion, indicating moderate weathering but no heavy erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural stone or gravel in landscaped areas, but can contrast with concrete or metal urban features.
